name: CMake
description: Cross-platform build system generator — produces Makefiles, Ninja files, Visual Studio projects, and more from a single CMakeLists.txt
when_to_use: The standard build system for most modern C and C++ projects

name: Ninja
description: Small, fast build system focused on speed — usually generated from CMake and consumed as the low-level backend
when_to_use: Faster alternative to make; most CMake projects should target Ninja

name: Meson
description: Modern build system designed for speed and ease of use — often used as a CMake alternative in GNOME and systemd
when_to_use: C/C++ projects that want better ergonomics than raw CMake

name: clang-format
description: LLVM project's code formatter for C, C++, Objective-C, and more — highly configurable via .clang-format
when_to_use: Any C/C++ project that wants automated formatting; preset styles match Google, LLVM, Mozilla, WebKit

name: clang-tidy
description: Extensible C++ linting tool from LLVM — catches bugs, style issues, and suggests modernization refactors
when_to_use: Any serious C/C++ project for static analysis and automated code modernization

name: Cppcheck
description: Static analysis tool for C/C++ code focused on low false-positive detection of bugs and undefined behaviour
when_to_use: Complementary to clang-tidy; especially good at finding memory leaks and null-pointer issues

name: Valgrind
description: Instrumentation framework for memory debugging, memory leak detection, and profiling — Memcheck, Helgrind, Callgrind
when_to_use: Finding memory bugs and leaks in C/C++ programs; requires Linux/macOS

name: Conan
description: C/C++ package manager that integrates with CMake, Meson, and Visual Studio — reproducible cross-platform dependency resolution
when_to_use: C/C++ projects tired of vendoring dependencies or reinventing package management

name: GoogleTest
description: Google's C++ testing and mocking framework — xUnit-style, fixtures, death tests, parameterized tests
when_to_use: Default choice for C++ unit testing

install.command: Add googletest as a submodule or via Conan

name: ccache
description: Compiler cache that speeds up recompilation by caching previous compilations and detecting when the same compilation is being done again
when_to_use: Any C/C++ project to dramatically speed up rebuild times, especially in CI pipelines

install.command: brew install ccache || sudo apt install ccache

name: Bear
description: Generates a compile_commands.json compilation database by intercepting build commands — required by clang-tidy and many LSP servers
when_to_use: When you need a compilation database for clang tooling but your build system does not generate one natively

install.command: brew install bear || sudo apt install bear

name: include-what-you-use
description: Analyzes #include directives in C/C++ source files to suggest adding missing includes and removing unnecessary ones
when_to_use: To clean up header dependencies, reduce build times, and enforce minimal includes in large codebases

install.command: brew install include-what-you-use || sudo apt install iwyu

name: AddressSanitizer
description: Runtime memory error detector built into GCC and Clang — detects buffer overflows, use-after-free, stack overflows, and memory leaks at runtime
when_to_use: During testing to catch memory bugs that static analysis misses; enable via compiler flags in debug builds

install.command: Included with GCC and Clang — enable via -fsanitize=address

name: Catch2
description: Header-only C++ testing framework with natural language-style test cases, BDD macros, and micro-benchmarking support
when_to_use: C++ projects wanting expressive test syntax with minimal setup — single header or CMake FetchContent

install.command: Add via CMake FetchContent or Conan: conan install catch2/3.x

name: doctest
description: Fastest feature-rich C++ single-header testing framework — compiles 20x faster than Catch2 with a similar API
when_to_use: When you want fast compilation times for tests; great for embedding test cases alongside production code

install.command: Add via CMake FetchContent or single header download

name: Doxygen
description: Documentation generator for C, C++, and other languages — extracts structured comments from source code and produces HTML, LaTeX, and man pages
when_to_use: Any C/C++ project that wants auto-generated API documentation from source code comments

install.command: brew install doxygen || sudo apt install doxygen

name: vcpkg
description: Microsoft's cross-platform C/C++ package manager — integrates with CMake and MSBuild, supports manifest mode for reproducible builds
when_to_use: Managing C/C++ dependencies with CMake integration, especially on Windows or cross-platform projects

name: xmake
description: Lua-based cross-platform build utility with built-in package management — modern alternative to CMake with simpler configuration syntax
when_to_use: C/C++ projects wanting a simpler build system than CMake with integrated dependency management

name: sccache
description: Shared compilation cache by Mozilla — like ccache but supports cloud storage backends (S3, GCS, Azure) for sharing cache across CI machines
when_to_use: CI pipelines where you want to share compiler cache across machines, or as a ccache replacement with cloud storage

install.command: brew install sccache || cargo install sccache

name: cpplint
description: Google's C++ style checker — enforces Google C++ Style Guide rules including naming conventions, header guards, and whitespace
when_to_use: C/C++ projects following Google style guide, or as a lightweight complement to clang-tidy
